2605.21362.title: LASH: Adaptive Semantic Hybridization for Black-Box Jailbreaking of Large Language Models
2605.21362.paper_summary: Jailbreak attacks expose a persistent gap between the intended safety behavior of aligned large language models and their behavior under adversarial prompting. Existing automated methods are increasingly effective but each commits to a single attack family (e.g., one refinement loop, one tree search, one mutation space, or one strategy library) and no single family dominates: the best-performing method shifts across target models and harm categories, suggesting complementary strengths that per-prompt composition could exploit. We introduce LASH (LLM Adaptive Semantic Hybridization), a black-box framework that treats outputs from multiple base attacks as reusable seed prompts and adaptively composes them for each target request. Given a seed pool, LASH searches over seed subsets and softmax-normalized mixture weights; a composition module synthesizes a single candidate prompt, and a derivative-free genetic optimizer updates the weights using black-box target feedback and a two-stage fitness function combining keyword-based refusal detection with LLM-judge scoring. On JailbreakBench, which contains 100 harmful prompts across 10 categories, we evaluate LASH on six common target models. LASH achieves an average attack success rate of 84.5% under keyword-based evaluation and 74.5% under two-stage evaluation, where responses are first filtered for refusals and then scored by an LLM judge for whether they substantively fulfill the original harmful request. LASH outperforms five state-of-the-art baselines on both metrics with only 30 mean target queries. LASH also remains competitive under three defense mechanisms and induces more success-like internal representations. These results suggest that adaptive composition across heterogeneous jailbreak strategies is a promising direction for black-box red-teaming.
2605.21362.paper_summary_zh: 監獄破解攻擊揭示了對齊的大型語言模型的預期安全行為與其在對抗性提示下的行為之間持續存在的差距。現有的自動化方法越來越有效，但每種方法僅專注於單一攻擊類別（例如，一個精煉迴圈、一個樹搜索、一個變異空間或一個策略庫），且沒有單一類別佔主導地位：最佳表現的方法在目標模型和傷害類別之間變化，這表明每個提示的組合可以利用互補的優勢。我們引入了LASH（LLM自適應語義混合），這是一個黑箱框架，將來自多個基礎攻擊的輸出視為可重用的種子提示，並根據每個目標請求自適應地組合它們。給定一個種子池，LASH在種子子集和softmax正規化的混合權重上進行搜索；組合模塊合成一個單一的候選提示，而無導數的遺傳優化器使用黑箱目標反饋和一個結合基於關鍵字的拒絕檢測與LLM評分的兩階段適應度函數來更新權重。在包含100個有害提示的JailbreakBench上，我們在六個常見目標模型上評估了LASH。LASH在基於關鍵字的評估下達到了84.5%的平均攻擊成功率，而在兩階段評估下則為74.5%，其中響應首先被過濾以檢查拒絕，然後由LLM評審進行評分，以判斷其是否實質上滿足原始的有害請求。LASH在這兩個指標上都超越了五個最先進的基準，僅用30個平均目標查詢。LASH在三種防禦機制下仍然保持競爭力，並產生了更多類似成功的內部表示。這些結果表明，跨異質監獄破解策略的自適應組合是一個有前景的黑箱紅隊方向。
2605.21362.author: Abdullah Al Nomaan Nafi et.al.
2605.21362.authors: Abdullah Al Nomaan Nafi, Fnu Suya, Swarup Bhunia, Prabuddha Chakraborty
